message:
fcp-sdk/contract: normalize ConnectorApp::app_contract's connector_id to self.id() instead of trusting describe()

`ConnectorApp::app_contract` composes the published contract from
three sources: the runtime-reported contract introspection, the
connector's author-supplied `describe()` metadata, and a capability
catalog derived from the two. The previous implementation used
`describe()`'s `connector_id` as the published `ConnectorDescriptor`
verbatim. That field is author-owned, so a connector whose
`describe()` disagreed with `self.id()` would publish a contract
under an ID different from the one the runtime resolves it by — at
best confusing, at worst exploitable (an attacker shipping a
connector that publishes under a well-known third-party ID).

Fix: right after `let mut description = self.describe();`, overwrite
`description.connector_id = self.id().clone();` so the published
contract is normalized to the runtime identity. Added a short
comment explaining the invariant.

Add a regression test `MismatchedDescriptorConnector` that implements
`ConnectorApp` by delegating every method to an inner
`ContractTestConnector` except `describe()`, which returns a
descriptor whose `connector_id` differs from the inner's `id()`.
Call `app_contract()` and assert the returned
`ConnectorAppContract.description.connector_id == self.id()`, i.e.
the runtime ID won. The `impl_fcp_sealed!` macro is applied to the
new type so it satisfies the sealed-trait requirement on
`ConnectorApp`.

No change to the introspection, describe, or capability-catalog
code paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
